behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, .alexignore, the required headers/sections (Next.js Development Guide, Git Workflow, Build Commands, Testing, Linting and Types), and the 5000-char threshold.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes desired end state (AGENTS.md as file, CLAUDE.md readable with same content, .alexignore contains both) without prescribing symlink commands or implementation steps.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: test_claude_md_is_symlink asserts CLAUDE.md must be a symlink pointing to 'AGENTS.md', but instruction.md only says CLAUDE.md 'should still be readable and return the same content' — a valid alternative fix (copying the file) would fail this test.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests execute subprocess cat, use os.readlink, Path.is_symlink, and check file-system behavior, not just text matching.

test_not_tautological.reason: f2p tests require actual file creation, symlink creation, and .alexignore edits; a stub cannot satisfy them.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All required behaviors (AGENTS.md content, CLAUDE.md readability, .alexignore contents) are covered by tests.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with section comments for pass_to_pass and fail_to_pass with descriptive names and docstrings.

no_typos.reason: No typos observed in filenames or paths.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names relevant files but this is inherent to the task domain; no buggy line/implementation is leaked.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: The .alexignore edit is adding one line (AGENTS.md), a localized change, not a full-file rewrite.
